rules.title: Label must be a semantic <label> associated to the input
rules.description: The .text-input__label element must be a <label> with a for attribute that matches the id of the .text-input__input element.
rules.remediation: Add for='field-id' to <label class='text-input__label'>. Add id='field-id' to <input class='text-input__input'>. The values must match.

rules.title: Required field must have aria-required on the input
rules.description: When a field is required, the <input> must carry aria-required='true'. Any visual asterisk (*) must have aria-hidden='true'.
rules.remediation: Add aria-required='true' to the <input> when required. Wrap any asterisk in a <span aria-hidden='true'>.

rules.title: Focus indicator must not be suppressed
rules.description: The .text-input__field::after underline provides the focus indicator. Overriding it with display:none, visibility:hidden, or transform:scaleX(0) permanently in CSS removes the focus indicator and violates WCAG 2.4.7.
rules.remediation: Do not override .text-input__field::after to hide the focus indicator. The native <input> outline is intentionally removed and replaced by the ::after underline.

rules.title: Error state must have aria-invalid and aria-describedby on input
rules.description: When .text-input--error is applied, the <input> must have aria-invalid='true'. If an error message is present, the input must have aria-describedby pointing to the error message element's id.
rules.check: .text-input--error input[aria-invalid='true'] and input[aria-describedby] when error message present
rules.remediation: Add aria-invalid='true' to the input when error state is active. Add aria-describedby='error-id' when an error message element exists. The error message element must have the matching id.

rules.title: No hardcoded visual values in component CSS
rules.description: Component CSS must not use hardcoded hex, rgba, pixel sizes, or font values outside of token fallbacks in var().
rules.check: No raw color, size, or typography values outside var(--text-input-*, fallback) patterns
rules.remediation: Replace hardcoded values with component-level tokens (--text-input-*). Fallbacks inside var() are acceptable pending token.css update.

rules.title: RTL must use dir attribute, not hardcoded CSS direction
rules.description: RTL layout must be triggered by dir='rtl' on the .text-input element or an ancestor. Do not add padding-left/padding-right overrides in component CSS for RTL.
rules.check: No hardcoded padding-left or padding-right in .text-input* selectors for RTL. CSS uses padding-inline-start/end.
rules.remediation: Set dir='rtl' on the .text-input element or an ancestor. The browser and logical CSS properties handle inline direction automatically.

rules.title: Disabled state must use class and native disabled attribute
rules.description: Disabled text input must have both .text-input--disabled on the root (for CSS) and disabled attribute on the <input> (for AT and browser behavior).
rules.remediation: Add .text-input--disabled to the root .text-input div AND add disabled attribute to the <input> element.

rules.title: Read-only state must use class and native readonly attribute
rules.description: Read-only text input must have both .text-input--readonly on the root (for CSS) and readonly attribute on the <input> (for AT and browser behavior).
rules.remediation: Add .text-input--readonly to the root .text-input div AND add readonly attribute to the <input> element.

rules.title: Input element must have type attribute
rules.description: The <input> element should have an explicit type attribute appropriate for the field's purpose. Omitting type defaults to 'text' but explicit declaration is recommended for clarity.
rules.remediation: Add type='text' (or appropriate type: email, tel, search, url, number, password) to the <input> element.

rules.title: Input element should have name attribute
rules.description: The <input> element should have a name attribute for form submission. Missing name prevents the field value from being included in form data.
rules.remediation: Add a name attribute to the <input> element.

rules.title: Component tokens should be defined in token.css
rules.description: The --text-input-* token definitions are currently only used as fallbacks in text-input.css. They should be formally defined in the global token.css under a Text Input tokens section.
rules.check: token.css contains all --text-input-* token definitions listed in analysis.md
rules.remediation: Add a '/* Text Input tokens */' section to token.css with all tokens listed in analysis.md Missing Tokens.

rules.title: Error message element must not be inside .text-input__field
rules.description: Error message text must be placed outside .text-input__field. It belongs at the form-field assembly layer. Placing it inside the field creates layout and AT issues.
rules.remediation: Move error message element to after .text-input__field as a sibling, or delegate to the form-field assembly component.
